reasoning: The 200m butterfly long course world record has been held by Liu Zige since October 2009 (2:01.81). As confirmed by the Wikipedia revision as of November 4, 2025, this record remains standing. The only active swimmer who has come close is Summer McIntosh, who swam a 2:01.99 in July 2025, just 0.18 seconds off the record. Regan Smith is the next closest active competitor but is about three seconds behind. Consequently, the record is only realistically threatened by McIntosh. The primary timeframe under consideration falls between November 2025 and February 2026. This period is historically the short-course season for elite swimming, headlined by the Short Course World Championships in December 2025. While there are some long course meets during this window—such as the US Open (Dec 3-6) and early Pro Swim Series events in early 2026—these are mid-season training meets where elite swimmers do not typically taper, making world records exceedingly rare. Given that the closest taper events (like national trials) usually occur from March onward, the probability of the record falling before February 2026 is extremely low. Thus, the confidence that Liu Zige will remain the record holder remains exceptionally high across all three resolution dates, slightly decreasing over time to account for the expanding 'at-risk' events window.
